feature: Login from Home page and transition to authenticated Home state

source.notes: Imported from Azure DevOps work item 6399

observedSteps: User Story As a customer, I want to open the Login page from the Home page and sign in with my credentials so that I can access my authenticated account experience and the additional features available after login.
Application Context This application is a single page application (SPA). The Login page is not directly accessed by URL. The user begins on the Home page at:
The Login page/view is opened by clicking the person/profile button on the Home page.
After successful authentication, the application returns to the Home page in an authenticated state.

Entry Conditions Launch the application. Navigate to http://localhost/washtabui/home . Ensure the Home page loads successfully in guest state. Open the Login page by clicking the person/profile button. Login Page Elements The Login page contains:
Username field Password field Login button Forgot password link or navigation Password masking behavior These controls should be modeled as Login page elements in the Login page POM/resource.

Behavior Rules The Login page should open only through the Home page person/profile button in this workflow. Username and Password are required fields unless business behavior states otherwise. Password input should be masked by default. Valid credentials should authenticate the user successfully. Invalid credentials should not authenticate the user. Failed login should keep the user on the Login page or in the unauthenticated state, with the expected error feedback displayed. Successful login should transition the application back to the Home page in authenticated state. Leading and trailing spaces in entered values should be handled according to expected business behavior and must be validated. Forgot password navigation should open the supported recovery flow or destination. Validation Expectations The workflow should validate:

Login page opens from the Home page. Username, Password, and Login button are visible and interactive. Password is masked by default. Successful login transitions the user to authenticated Home state. Invalid login displays error feedback and does not authenticate the user. Required field validations are displayed correctly. Forgot password navigation works correctly. Whitespace handling behaves according to business expectation. Transition Expectations After successful login:

The user should no longer remain in the guest Home state. The Home page should display authenticated-user UI. The person/profile button should be replaced by the logged-in user name. Additional authenticated-only features should become available. POM Reuse Guidance Use the Home page resource for opening the Login page from Home and for validating post-login authenticated Home state. Use the Login page resource for entering credentials, clicking Login, verifying Login-specific validations, and forgot password behavior. Successful login tests should reuse both Home and Login resources in sequence: Home keyword to open Login Login keyword(s) to authenticate Home keyword(s) to verify authenticated state Acceptance Criteria Given the user is on the Home page in guest state, when the user clicks the person/profile button, then the Login page should open. Given the user is on the Login page, when the user enters a valid username and valid password and clicks Login, then the user should be authenticated successfully and the application should return to the Home page in authenticated state. Given the user is on the Login page, when the user enters an invalid username or invalid password and clicks Login, then login should fail, an error message should be displayed, and the user should remain unauthenticated. Given the user is on the Login page, when the user clicks Login without entering required fields, then required field validation should be shown and login should not proceed. Given the user is on the Login page, when the user leaves Username blank and enters Password and clicks Login, then Username validation should be shown and login should not proceed. Given the user is on the Login page, when the user enters Username and leaves Password blank and clicks Login, then Password validation should be shown and login should not proceed. Given the user is on the Login page, when the user enters a password, then the password should be masked by default. Given the user is on the Login page, when the user enters values with leading or trailing spaces and clicks Login, then the application should handle the values according to expected business behavior and the observed result should be validated. Given the user is on the Login page, when the user clicks Forgot Password, then the supported recovery destination or recovery workflow should open. Given the user logs in successfully, when the application returns to Home, then the logged-in user name and authenticated-only features should be visible. Generation Guidance Generate:

login entry tests from Home successful login tests invalid credential tests required field validation tests password masking tests forgot password navigation tests post-login transition tests that verify authenticated Home state Prefer:
Home resource for navigation into Login and post-login Home validation Login resource for form actions and form validations explicit transition assertions rather than only action completion
